title: RNAse free 4% Paraformaldehye in .1M PB preparation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"a62b4","text":"Fixative for perfusion with down-stream RNA preservation and detection.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Add 400 mL of Nuclease-free water to a 1000 mL, beaker
Add stir bar in beaker and set on hot plate at 50 °C
Add 5 drops (500ul) of 10N NaOH (from 60ml dropper) Sodium Hydroxide, 10.0N Thomas Scientific Catalog #C871W32 (this makes the solution basic to help dissolve the paraformaldehyde)
Weigh 20 g of Paraformaldehyde and add to MilliQ water. (Stir about 01:00:00 or until all parafromaldehyde is dissolved and solution is clear) Paraformaldehyde Contributed by users Catalog #19200
Make 1 Molarity (M) Phosphate buffer at PH 7.4
Top off to 500ml with MilliQ water.
Filter solution through .2um CA member filter. Corning™ Disposable Vacuum Filter/Storage Systems Fisher Scientific Catalog #09-761-5

Label container with 'RNAse free 4% paraformaldehyed in .1M PB' and date. Store at 4 °C, for up to 1 week. (Can also be aliquoted into Falcon Tube (50 mL) Fischer Scientific and stored at -20 °C for 3 months.)

To make 100 mL, 1M PB at PH 7.4 add 19 mL, 1M Sodium phosphate Monobasic plus 81 mL, 1M Sodium Dibasic dihydrate (store as stock at Room temperature ) Sodium Phosphate Dibasic Dihydrate (USP/FCC/EP/BP), Fisher Chemical Fisher Scientific Catalog #S472-500 Sodium Phosphate Dibasic Dihydrate (USP/FCC/EP/BP), Fisher Chemical Fisher Scientific Catalog #S472-500
Add 50 mL, 1M PB PH 7.4 to paraformaldehyde solution.
Test PH and adjust solution to final desired 7.4pH using HCl or NaOH.
creator: Monique Copeland
